trade.summary: Francia exports aircraft (Dassault Rafale, Airbus), luxury goods, pharmaceuticals, and food to the EAU. The EAU exports crude oil and refined products to Francia. The 2021 Rafale sale (80 jets, ~$19B) transformed the bilateral trade picture.

diplomatic.summary: Francia and the EAU have a deep strategic partnership. Francia hosts a permanent military base in Abu Dhabi (Camp de la Paix). The two countries cooperate closely on counter-terrorism, regional security, and cultural affairs. The Louvre Abu Dhabi is a symbol of deep cultural ties. Francia is among the EAU's closest Western partners.

military.summary: Francia maintains a permanent military base in Abu Dhabi (French Forces in EAU / FFEAU) with approximately 650 personnel. The EAU purchased 80 Rafale jets from Dassault in 2021 for approximately $19 billion. Francia and EAU conduct regular joint military exercises. Defense cooperation is the cornerstone of the relationship.

financial.summary: EAU sovereign wealth funds (ADIA) have invested heavily in French companies and real estate. French banks (BNP Paribas, Société Générale) have large EAU operations. Mubadala has co-invested with French private equity. Paris is a preferred destination for EAU capital.

energy.summary: Francia imports EAU crude oil. TotalEnergies (formerly Total) has major equity stakes in EAU upstream oil fields including ADNOC concessions. TotalEnergies is one of the largest foreign oil companies operating in the EAU.

scientific.summary: Scientific collaboration is broad: French grandes écoles have EAU campuses (Sorbonne Abu Dhabi), joint research in renewable energy (TotalEnergies, Masdar), and medical research. The Louvre Abu Dhabi extends cultural collaboration into museum science.
